title: Protocol: clinical trial access in conflict-affected African states
body: This cross-sectional registry study will compare registered interventional trial activity between nine conflict-affected African states and five stable comparators using ClinicalTrials.gov as the data source. We will query ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for all interventional studies with at least one site in each prespecified country, calculating trials per million population and extracting condition-specific counts. The primary metric is the Conflict Exclusion Score, defined as the ratio of stable comparator per-capita trial density to conflict-state per-capita trial density. Secondary analyses will examine condition-specific trial volumes for HIV, malaria, tuberculosis, maternal health, nutrition, PTSD, and mental health, together with within-country geographic concentration in the Democratic Republic of Congo as a case study. All queries will be scripted, rate limited, and cached for full reproducibility, with results summarised in tables and figures. No patient-level data will be used. Limitations include dependence on a single registry, country-level aggregation that masks sub-national variation, and incomplete capture of humanitarian or locally registered studies.

sentences.text: This cross-sectional registry study will compare registered interventional trial activity between nine conflict-affected African states and five more stable comparators.
We will query ClinicalTrials.gov for all interventional studies with at least one site in the prespecified countries and calculate trials per million population.
The primary metric is the Conflict Exclusion Score, defined as the ratio of comparator to conflict-state per-capita trial density.

Secondary analyses will examine condition-specific counts for HIV, malaria, tuberculosis, maternal health, nutrition, PTSD, and mental health, together with within-country concentration in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.
All queries will be scripted and cached for reproducibility, and results summarised in tables and figures.
No patient-level data will be used.
Limitations include registry dependence, country-level aggregation, and incomplete capture of humanitarian or locally registered studies.

outsideNote.data: ClinicalTrials.gov API v2, 9 conflict states, 5 stable comparators, 6 conditions

ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.
